name: Pumpkin Empanadas (Jacqueline Higuera Mcmahan)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the first four ingredients (flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt).
2. Cut in the shortening and butter using a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Combine the brandy and milk, and drizzle over the flour mixture, stirring with a fork to distribute. Do not over-blend.
4. Form into a flat disc, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill for 30 minutes.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees.
6. To make the filling, blend together the pumpkin, brown sugar, spices (cinnamon, anise leaves, and nutmeg), egg, and vanilla.
7. When the dough is chilled, roll out thinly on a floured board or pastry cloth.
8. Cut into 4- or 5-inch circles. Place about 2 tablespoons of filling on the lower half of each dough circle.
9. Fold over the top half and press the edges together with a fork.
10. Brush the tops with egg wash and place on a baking sheet.
11. Bake for 15 to 18 minutes, or until golden.
12. While still warm, sprinkle with the cinnamon sugar mixture.
13. These can be baked up to one day ahead. Before serving, warm in an oven at 350 degrees for 8 minutes.
Makes 14 to 16 empanadas.
